title: Xenial Ethiopian Lentils Misir Wot
description: A floral, shaggy, festive misir wot with creamy stracciatella swirl and char‑grilled tomato accents, low‑glycemic and perfect for celebratory meals.
instructions: Rinse red lentils thoroughly until water runs clear.
In a medium saucepan combine lentils, water, and a pinch of salt; bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
While lentils cook, heat a grill pan over medium‑high heat and char the diced tomato until slightly blackened.
In a separate skillet heat olive oil and sauté onion, garlic, and ginger until fragrant and translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add smoked paprika, berbere spice, tomato paste, and the char‑grilled tomatoes to the skillet; cook for 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Stir in the cooked lentils and vegetable broth; simmer for another 10 minutes until the mixture thickens, then fold in fresh cilantro.
Spoon the misir wot onto serving plates and dollop generous amounts of stracciatella cheese on top.
Garnish with extra cilantro leaves and serve immediately with injera or a low‑glycemic side.
